title: CBIC Clarifies GST Jurisdiction for Migrated Taxpayers
content:
# CBIC Clarifies GST Jurisdiction for Migrated Taxpayers

Ek important update hai. Government ne "Jurisdictional Competence in GST Proceedings Following Migration of Taxable Persons" ke liye clarification announce kiya hai.

Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) ne Circular No. 255/01/2026-GST dated June 25, 2026, issue karke GST proceedings mein jurisdictional competence ko clear kiya hai, especially jab koi taxable person apna Principal Place of Business change karke doosre GST jurisdiction mein migrate karta hai.

**Key Clarifications:**

*   **Validity of Prior Actions:** Agar transferor (previous) jurisdictional authority ne migration se pehle koi action (jaise investigation, audit, show cause notice, adjudication, appeal) validly initiate kiya tha, toh woh legally valid rahega, even after the taxpayer migrates.
*   **Transferor's Lost Authority:** Taxpayer ke migrate hone ke baad, transferor officer fresh proceedings initiate ya existing proceedings ko continue nahi kar sakta.
*   **Transferee's Responsibility:** Transferee (new) jurisdictional authority ko pending proceedings ko usi stage se take over karna hoga jis stage par woh migration ke waqt the.
*   **Consequential Actions:** Transferee authority previous actions ko implement karegi, subsequent proceedings conduct karegi, appellate authorities ke saamne matters ko defend karegi, aur zaroorat padne par appeals bhi file karegi.
*   **New Issues:** Agar transferor officer ko migration ke baad koi naya issue pata chalta hai, toh use transferee officer ko inform karna hoga further action ke liye.

Yeh clarification jurisdictional ambiguities ko remove karega aur GST Act ke uniform implementation ko ensure karega.

## Read the Official Document
* [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://taxo.online/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Circular-No-255-01-2026.pdf)

*Published on: 28 June 2026*
